adult.dataset_description: UCI Census Income dataset. Predicts whether annual income exceeds $50K based on demographic features. Contains 8 nominal categorical columns (workclass, education, marital_status, occupation, relationship, race, sex, native_country) with no natural ordering except education (but education_num already encodes this). No true ID columns.
must_do: encode_categorical with ordinal for tree-based efficiency
adult.notes: Dataset domain: demographics/census. Categorical nature: mostly nominal (no natural ordering). High-cardinality columns: native_country (41 values), occupation (14), education (16). ML model encoding guidance: Ordinal encoding is efficient and appropriate, same as Random Forest. GBM builds trees sequentially and benefits from compact feature representation. High-cardinality columns are better encoded as ordinal to avoid sparse feature matrices.
